What is next for Bank Of America after Q1 earnings?
Berkshire CEO Abel sold stocks managed by ex-portfolio manager Combs, WSJ reports
items.content: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has divested stocks once overseen by Todd Combs. Combs departed Berkshire in December for JPMorgan Chase. This move signals a significant shift in Berkshire's investment portfolio under Abel, who took over as CEO in January. Warren Buffett continues as Chairman. Berkshire is expected to reveal more details on its equity holdings soon.
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has divested stocks once overseen by Todd Combs. Combs departed Berkshire in December for JPMorgan Chase. This move signals a significant shift in Berkshire's investme...

items.title: 5 world market themes for the week ahead
items.content: Markets are optimistic as U.S. President Trump signals an end to the Iran war. Talks may resume soon. However, upcoming economic data could show slowing business and rising prices. Investors also await the Federal Reserve nominee's Congressional hearing. Tesla earnings are also a key focus for U.S. corporations this week.

Immune cells have a surprising role in exercise endurance
Can sparkling water boost metabolism and help with weight loss?
items.content: Sparkling water is often seen as a simple, healthy drink—but could it also help with weight loss? New research suggests it may slightly boost how the body processes blood sugar and energy. However, the effect is very small, meaning it’s no substitute for diet and exercise.

items.title: Scientists supercharge immune cells to destroy cancer more effectively
items.content: Researchers have found a way to make cancer-killing immune cells more powerful and precise. By adding specific signaling components, they boosted the cells’ readiness to attack tumors. Surprisingly, briefly suppressing the cells with a drug before use made them even more effective later. The approach could help create safer, stronger next-gen cancer treatments.

items.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
items.content: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.
